enrichment.short_description: Resend is a developer-first email API and SMTP platform for sending transactional and marketing emails, built around React Email and modern deliverability tooling.
enrichment.starting_price: Free (3,000 emails/month); paid plans start at $20/month
enrichment.company_size: Small team (around 46 people per company data)
enrichment.seo_title: Resend Review 2026: Pricing, Features and Alternatives
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Resend is a developer-focused email API for transactional and marketing email. See 2026 pricing, features, pros and cons, and FAQs in this in-depth review.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Resend is a developer-focused email API and SMTP platform founded in 2022 and headquartered in San Francisco, California, built by a small team backed by seed investors including Figma's Dylan Field, Vercel's Guillermo Rauch, and Supabase's Paul Copplestone, and later an $18 million Series A led by Andreessen Horowitz in December 2024, bringing total funding to roughly $21.5 million. Resend positions itself as the email API for developers, offering a RESTful API, SMTP relay, and official SDKs for languages including Node.js, Python, PHP, Ruby, Go, Java, and Rust, alongside deep integration with React Email, the open-source React component library for building emails that Resend also maintains. The platform supports transactional email (order confirmations, password resets, one-time codes) and, on a separate contact-based pricing track, marketing broadcasts and newsletters. Pricing runs on a freemium model: the Free plan allows 3,000 emails per month capped at 100 per day on a single domain; the Pro plan costs $20 per month for 50,000 emails or $35 per month for 100,000 emails; the Scale plan spans $90 to $1,150 per month for 100,000 to 2.5 million emails with declining per-email overage rates and support for up to 1,000 sending domains, dedicated IPs, and Slack-based support; and Enterprise plans are custom-priced for senders exceeding roughly 3 million emails per month. All tiers include core deliverability infrastructure such as DKIM, SPF, and DMARC authentication, multi-region sending, open and click tracking, batch sending, inbound email parsing, and webhooks, and Resend holds SOC 2 Type II and GDPR compliance along with multi-factor authentication and granular API key permissions. The product is popular among startups and SaaS companies building on modern JavaScript and TypeScript stacks, largely because of its tight coupling with React Email and its emphasis on a clean developer experience, clear logs, and fast API response times, as opposed to legacy enterprise ESPs. Notable limitations include the lack of a fully self-serve high-volume enterprise tier, the need to contact sales for the largest sending volumes, and a 2024 pricing change that roughly doubled some Scale-tier prices, for example the 200,000-email tier moved from $80 to $160 per month, which drew criticism from some existing customers. Resend competes with providers like SendGrid, Postmark, Mailgun, and Amazon SES, differentiating primarily through developer experience, React Email integration, and modern API design rather than being the cheapest option at scale.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Resend is an email API and SMTP platform built specifically for developers, founded in 2022 and headquartered in San Francisco. It combines a RESTful API, SMTP relay, official SDKs, and the React Email component library into a single toolkit for sending both transactional and marketing email from modern application stacks.

Backed by notable angel investors from Figma, Vercel, and Supabase, and later an Andreessen Horowitz-led Series A, Resend has grown into a widely used alternative to legacy email service providers, particularly among teams already working in JavaScript and TypeScript.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Resend's core feature set covers the full transactional email lifecycle: a RESTful API and SMTP relay for sending, inbound email parsing for receiving, batch sending for volume, and webhooks and open/click tracking for monitoring delivery and engagement.

Deliverability is handled through built-in DKIM, SPF, and DMARC authentication, multi-region sending infrastructure, and optional dedicated IPs on higher tiers, while security and compliance are covered by SOC 2 Type II certification, GDPR compliance, multi-factor authentication, and granular API key permissions.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Resend uses a freemium, usage-based pricing model split across four tiers. The Free plan covers 3,000 emails per month (100/day, 1 domain). Pro costs $20/month for 50,000 emails or $35/month for 100,000 emails. Scale ranges from $90 to $1,150/month for 100,000 to 2.5 million emails, adding dedicated IPs, 1,000 domains, and Slack support. Enterprise is custom-priced for 3M+ emails per month.

A separate marketing email track bills by number of contacts rather than emails sent, running from roughly $40/month up to $650/month depending on list size for teams sending newsletters and broadcasts through the same account.

features.title: RESTful API and SMTP relay
features.description: Send transactional and marketing email through a modern REST API or a standard SMTP relay, whichever fits the existing stack.

features.title: React Email integration
features.description: Build emails as React components using the open-source React Email library, which Resend also maintains.

features.title: Official SDKs
features.description: First-party SDKs for Node.js, Python, PHP, Ruby, Go, Java, and Rust simplify integration.

features.title: Inbound email parsing
features.description: Receive and parse inbound email, not just send outbound messages.

features.title: Batch sending and webhooks
features.description: Send emails in bulk and receive real-time delivery, bounce, and engagement events via webhooks.

features.title: Deliverability tooling
features.description: Built-in DKIM, SPF, and DMARC authentication plus optional dedicated IPs with automatic warmup on the Scale plan.

features.title: Open and click tracking
features.description: Track opens and link clicks across sent campaigns and transactional messages.

features.title: SOC 2 Type II and GDPR compliance
features.description: Enterprise-grade security and compliance posture with MFA and granular API key permissions.

pros: Clean, modern developer experience with well-documented API and SDKs
Deep React Email integration for building emails in JSX
Generous free tier for small projects (3,000 emails/month)
Fast setup for transactional email in Node.js/TypeScript stacks
Strong deliverability tooling included out of the box
cons: Enterprise-scale pricing requires contacting sales
2024 price increases on higher Scale tiers upset some long-time customers
Marketing/broadcast features are billed separately from transactional email
Newer platform than incumbents like SendGrid or Mailgun, so track record is shorter
Daily send cap on the free plan (100 emails/day) limits testing at scale

faqs.question: Is Resend free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, Resend offers a free tier covering 3,000 emails per month, capped at 100 emails per day on one domain.

faqs.question: What is Resend used for?
faqs.answer: Resend is used to send transactional email (receipts, password resets, OTPs) and marketing broadcasts from applications via API or SMTP.

faqs.question: Does Resend integrate with React Email?
faqs.answer: Yes, Resend created and maintains React Email and offers native integration for building emails as React components.

faqs.question: How much does Resend cost?
faqs.answer: Paid plans start at $20/month for Pro (50,000 emails), scaling up to $1,150/month for Scale (2.5 million emails), with custom Enterprise pricing above that.

faqs.question: Does Resend support SMTP?
faqs.answer: Yes, alongside its REST API, Resend offers a standard SMTP relay for sending email.

faqs.question: When was Resend founded and where is it based?
faqs.answer: Resend was founded in 2022 and is headquartered in San Francisco, California.

faqs.question: Does Resend offer dedicated IPs?
faqs.answer: Yes, dedicated IPs are available as a $30/month add-on for Scale plan customers sending more than 3,000 emails per day.

faqs.question: Is Resend SOC 2 and GDPR compliant?
faqs.answer: Yes, Resend holds SOC 2 Type II certification and is GDPR compliant.

use_cases.title: Transactional email for SaaS products
use_cases.description: Send order confirmations, password resets, and one-time codes reliably via API or SMTP.

use_cases.title: Marketing newsletters and broadcasts
use_cases.description: Send contact-based newsletters and campaigns using Resend's separate marketing pricing track.

use_cases.title: Product notification systems
use_cases.description: Build automated, high-deliverability notification flows integrated directly into application code.

enrichment.short_description: Resilio Sync is a peer-to-peer file synchronization tool built on BitTorrent protocol technology that syncs files directly between devices without routing through the cloud.
enrichment.pricing_model: Freemium with one-time license (individuals) and custom subscription pricing (business)
enrichment.starting_price: Free (Sync Home); Home Pro is a one-time $59.99 license
enrichment.company_size: Small-to-mid-size team; acquired by Nasuni in March 2026
enrichment.seo_title: Resilio Sync Review 2026: P2P File Sync Pricing and Features
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Resilio Sync uses BitTorrent-based peer-to-peer technology to sync files across devices. See 2026 pricing for Home and Business plans, features, and FAQs.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Resilio Sync is a peer-to-peer file synchronization tool that grew out of BitTorrent Inc.'s original BitTorrent Sync product; the technology and team were spun out into an independent company, Resilio Inc. (legally Rainberry, Inc.), in mid-2016 under former BitTorrent CEO Eric Klinker, headquartered in San Francisco with additional offices in Connecticut and Warsaw, Poland. Unlike cloud-storage-based sync tools such as Dropbox or Google Drive, Resilio Sync transfers files directly between a user's own devices, and for teams between servers, using an optimized version of the BitTorrent protocol, so files never have to pass through or be stored on a third-party cloud, which appeals to users prioritizing speed, large-file handling, and data sovereignty. The product is split into two lines: Resilio Sync for individuals and Resilio Sync Business, the latter now being transitioned toward a newer platform called Resilio Active Everywhere for teams and enterprises. On the individual side, Sync Home is free and supports basic peer-to-peer folder syncing across a limited number of devices, while Sync Home Pro is sold as a one-time, non-subscription license priced at $59.99 that unlocks selective sync, sync to cloud or NAS storage, file versioning, and use across all of a single person's devices without a recurring fee. Resilio Sync Business, by contrast, is subscription-based and sales-gated: pricing is not published on the website and requires contacting Resilio's sales team, with plans historically starting around a few dollars per user per month depending on volume and features such as centralized management, LDAP/Active Directory integration, and audit logging. In March 2026, Resilio was acquired by cloud file services company Nasuni, which is expected to fold Resilio's peer-to-peer sync technology into Nasuni's broader file infrastructure and Active Everywhere platform over time. Core use cases for Resilio Sync include syncing large media files such as video, photography, and CAD data between studios and freelancers, keeping NAS devices and laptops in sync without cloud storage costs, and providing offline-capable file access for field teams. Because it relies on peer-to-peer transfer, Resilio Sync's real-time performance depends on devices being online simultaneously, or relayed through Resilio's servers when they are not, which differs fundamentally from always-on cloud storage services and is an important distinction for buyers comparing it to Dropbox, Google Drive, or OneDrive.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Resilio Sync originated as BitTorrent Sync inside BitTorrent Inc. before being spun out as an independent company, Resilio, in 2016. It uses peer-to-peer BitTorrent-protocol technology to sync files directly between devices rather than routing them through a central cloud server.

The product line covers individuals (Sync Home and Sync Home Pro) and businesses (Sync Business, transitioning to Resilio Active Everywhere), with Resilio acquired by cloud file platform Nasuni in March 2026.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Resilio Sync supports selective folder sync, file versioning, and cross-platform apps for Windows, Mac, Linux, iOS, Android, and many NAS devices, enabling fast transfer of very large files without a cloud storage intermediary.
Business deployments add centralized management, LDAP/Active Directory integration, and audit logging, targeting organizations that need private, point-to-point file distribution across offices.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Individuals can use Sync Home for free with basic functionality, or purchase Sync Home Pro as a one-time $59.99 lifetime license covering unlimited devices for one person, with no recurring subscription.
Resilio Sync Business pricing is not published; it is a sales-gated, subscription-based product that requires contacting Resilio directly for a custom quote based on team size and required features.

features.title: Peer-to-peer (BitTorrent-protocol) file transfer
features.description: Syncs files directly between devices using an optimized BitTorrent protocol instead of routing through central cloud servers.

features.title: Selective sync and folder-level control
features.description: Choose exactly which folders and files sync to which devices.

features.title: NAS and server sync support
features.description: Works with a wide range of NAS devices and servers, not just desktop and mobile apps.

features.title: File versioning and conflict resolution
features.description: Keeps prior file versions and handles sync conflicts automatically.

features.title: Cross-platform apps
features.description: Available for Windows, Mac, Linux, iOS, Android, and NAS platforms.

features.title: One-time license option for individuals
features.description: Sync Home Pro is a non-subscription, one-time purchase rather than a recurring fee.

features.title: Business management features
features.description: Sync Business adds centralized management, LDAP/AD integration, and audit logging.

features.title: Cloud-free operation
features.description: Files can sync entirely without being stored on third-party cloud infrastructure.

pros: No recurring subscription needed for individual Home Pro license
Fast large-file transfers using P2P protocol, good for media-heavy workflows
Keeps files off third-party cloud storage for privacy and data-sovereignty reasons
Cross-platform support including NAS devices
Effective for syncing very large files and folders such as video and RAW photos
cons: Business pricing is not published and requires a sales conversation
Requires devices to be online, or relay servers, for real-time sync unlike always-on cloud storage
Acquired by Nasuni in 2026, creating uncertainty about the product's long-term roadmap
Less mainstream name recognition than Dropbox, Google Drive, or OneDrive
Folder-sharing model is less intuitive for non-technical users than typical cloud drives

faqs.question: What is Resilio Sync?
faqs.answer: Resilio Sync is a peer-to-peer file synchronization tool that syncs files directly between devices using BitTorrent-protocol technology, without requiring cloud storage.

faqs.question: Is Resilio Sync free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Sync Home is free with basic functionality; Sync Home Pro is a one-time $59.99 purchase for expanded features.

faqs.question: How is Resilio Sync different from Dropbox or Google Drive?
faqs.answer: Resilio Sync transfers files directly between devices peer-to-peer rather than storing a copy on third-party cloud servers.

faqs.question: How much does Resilio Sync Home Pro cost?
faqs.answer: Sync Home Pro is a one-time, non-subscription license priced at $59.99.

faqs.question: Does Resilio Sync work with NAS devices?
faqs.answer: Yes, Resilio Sync supports a wide range of NAS devices in addition to desktop and mobile platforms.

faqs.question: Who owns Resilio Sync now?
faqs.answer: Resilio was acquired by cloud file services company Nasuni in March 2026.

faqs.question: Is there a Resilio Sync business plan?
faqs.answer: Yes, Resilio Sync Business is a subscription product with custom, sales-gated pricing for teams.

faqs.question: Does Resilio Sync store my files in the cloud?
faqs.answer: No, by default Resilio Sync transfers files directly between devices rather than storing them on third-party cloud servers.

enrichment.short_description: Resource Guru is a cloud-based team scheduling and resource management tool that helps agencies, studios, and professional services teams book people, equipment, and rooms.
enrichment.seo_title: Resource Guru Review 2026: Pricing, Features and Alternatives
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Resource Guru is a team scheduling tool for booking people, equipment and rooms. Compare 2026 pricing plans, features, pros/cons and FAQs.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Resource Guru is a cloud-based resource scheduling and team management platform founded in 2011 and headquartered in London, United Kingdom, run by a lean team of roughly 19 to 25 people. It is built specifically for scheduling people, equipment, and meeting rooms in a single visual drag-and-drop calendar, targeting agencies, consultancies, professional services firms, and architecture and engineering studios that need to see who is available and avoid double-booking. Resource Guru offers three subscription tiers billed per person per month: Grasshopper at $5/month, or $4.16/month billed annually, includes unlimited projects and clients, personalized dashboards, custom filters, calendar sync, one free placeholder resource, and seven days of activity history; Blackbelt at $8/month, or $6.67/month billed annually, adds timesheets, customizable reports, project dates and milestones, project rates and budgets, ten custom fields, five free placeholders, and ninety days of activity history; and Master at $12/month, or $10/month billed annually, adds approval workflows, single sign-on, unlimited activity history and custom fields, phone support, and ten free placeholders. Non-human resources such as equipment or rooms are billed at a reduced rate of roughly $2.50 to $6 depending on plan rather than the full per-person price. All plans include a 30-day free trial that doesn't require a credit card, and customers on annual billing effectively get two months free compared to monthly billing; invoice-based payment is available for larger accounts of 10 or more users on annual plans. Resource Guru differentiates itself from broader project-management suites like Asana or Monday.com by focusing narrowly on resource scheduling and capacity planning rather than task tracking, which many users cite as a strength for clarity but a limitation if they want an all-in-one project management tool. Leave management, waiting-list and overbooking alerts, and a clash-detection calendar are core to the product. The company has remained an independently operated SaaS business, reported at several million dollars in annual recurring revenue, rather than pursuing large venture funding rounds, and it markets itself heavily on simplicity, fast onboarding, and a UI that scheduling teams can use without extensive training.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Resource Guru is a cloud-based scheduling tool founded in 2011 and based in London, purpose-built for booking people, equipment, and rooms on a single visual calendar rather than managing full project tasks.
It's aimed at agencies, consultancies, and professional services teams that need clear, fast visibility into who and what is available, with clash detection to prevent double-booking.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: The core is a drag-and-drop scheduling calendar supporting people, equipment, and meeting rooms, with leave management, custom fields, and calendar sync to Google, Outlook, and iCal.
Higher tiers add timesheets, project budgets and rates, approval workflows, and single sign-on, plus reporting and utilization dashboards for tracking team capacity.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Resource Guru has three per-user tiers: Grasshopper at $5/month ($4.16 annually), Blackbelt at $8/month ($6.67 annually), and Master at $12/month ($10 annually), each unlocking progressively more features.
Equipment and room resources are billed at a discounted rate compared to human users, and all plans include a 30-day free trial with no credit card required.

features.description: Visually schedule people, equipment, and rooms on one shared calendar.

features.title: Leave management
features.description: Track team availability and time off alongside project bookings.

features.description: Filter and match resources by skills, location, or other custom attributes.

features.description: Available on Blackbelt and above for tracking time against projects.

features.title: Project budgets, rates and milestones
features.description: Track project financials and key dates on Blackbelt and above.

features.title: Approval workflows and SSO
features.description: Available on the Master plan for larger, governance-focused teams.

features.title: Calendar sync
features.description: Sync bookings with Google Calendar, Outlook, and iCal.

features.title: Reporting and utilization dashboards
features.description: Customizable reports to track team capacity and utilization.

pros: Simple, purpose-built scheduling UI that's fast to learn
Affordable per-user pricing with a generous free trial
Handles people, equipment, and rooms in one calendar
Strong clash and overbooking prevention
Reduced pricing for non-human resources like equipment and rooms
cons: Not a full project management tool, no task boards or Gantt charts in the core product
SSO and approval workflows are locked to the top Master tier
Per-user pricing can add up for larger teams
Reporting and analytics depth is lighter than dedicated PM or BI suites
No native time-tracking beyond the Blackbelt-and-above timesheets

faqs.question: What is Resource Guru used for?
faqs.answer: Resource Guru is used for scheduling and booking people, equipment, and meeting rooms to avoid overbooking and manage team capacity.

faqs.question: How much does Resource Guru cost?
faqs.answer: Plans range from $5 to $12 per user per month billed monthly, or $4.16 to $10 per user per month billed annually.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, Resource Guru offers a 30-day free trial with no credit card required.

faqs.question: Does Resource Guru do timesheets?
faqs.answer: Yes, timesheets are included on the Blackbelt plan and above.

faqs.question: Can I schedule equipment and rooms, not just people?
faqs.answer: Yes, Resource Guru supports scheduling equipment and rooms as resources, billed at a lower rate than people.

faqs.question: Does Resource Guru support SSO?
faqs.answer: Yes, single sign-on is available on the Master plan.

faqs.question: Who is Resource Guru for?
faqs.answer: It's designed for agencies, consultancies, and professional services teams that need visual resource scheduling.

faqs.question: Can I cancel my Resource Guru subscription anytime?
faqs.answer: Yes, users can upgrade, downgrade, or cancel at any time with no setup fees.

enrichment.short_description: ResourceSpace is an open-source digital asset management (DAM) system that can be self-hosted for free or run as a managed cloud service by its maintainer, Montala.
enrichment.pricing_model: Free open-source self-hosted; paid managed cloud hosting tiers
enrichment.starting_price: Free (self-hosted, or free cloud tier with 10GB/10 users); paid cloud plans start around $358/month
enrichment.company_size: Small team; maintainer Montala Limited is 100% employee-owned
enrichment.seo_title: ResourceSpace Review 2026: Open-Source DAM Pricing and Features
enrichment.seo_meta_description: ResourceSpace is a free, open-source digital asset management system with optional managed cloud hosting. Compare 2026 pricing, features and FAQs.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: ResourceSpace is an open-source digital asset management (DAM) system originally created in 2006 by developers Dan Huby and Neale Hall while working as contractors for the charity Oxfam, which needed a better way to organize its media library than the expensive, clunky DAM tools available at the time; Oxfam subsequently released the software under an open-source license, and Huby went on to found Montala Limited, the UK company that now maintains ResourceSpace, provides professional services, and hosts the managed cloud version. Montala is headquartered in Watchfield, Oxfordshire, in the UK, is 100% employee-owned, operates as a certified B Corporation, and holds ISO 27001 certification and UK Crown Commercial Service supplier status. Because ResourceSpace is open source, there is no software license fee for self-hosting: organizations can download and run the full DAM platform on their own servers at no cost, with no per-user restrictions, and charges only apply to optional paid support or the managed cloud offering. For teams that don't want to self-host, ResourceSpace offers a free cloud tier with 10GB of storage and up to 10 users, plus paid cloud hosting tiers hosted in London Docklands data centers: Team Cloud around $358/month, roughly $5,108/year, for 25 users and 1-9TB storage; Business Cloud around $778/month, roughly $11,500/year, for 100 users plus optional public access and 3-15TB storage; Enterprise Cloud around $1,357/month, roughly $20,355/year, for 500 users, 8-20TB storage, and priority support; and a custom-quoted Platinum Cloud tier for unlimited users and 50TB-plus storage. All paid cloud tiers include AI-assisted tools such as automatic tagging, face detection, and natural-language search, along with SSO, API access, SFTP drop folders, a test or staging instance, and a dedicated server. ResourceSpace is used by universities, charities, museums, marketing teams, and enterprises to centrally store, tag, search, and control access to images, video, and documents, and it supports metadata schemas, watermarking, usage rights tracking, and integration via its open API. Its combination of a genuinely free, unrestricted open-source core with an optional fully managed hosting path distinguishes it from most commercial DAM vendors, such as Bynder or Widen, which are exclusively closed-source SaaS products with per-user licensing.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: ResourceSpace was created in 2006 by Dan Huby and Neale Hall while contracting for the charity Oxfam, which released the resulting DAM software under an open-source license. Huby later founded Montala Limited, an employee-owned UK company that maintains ResourceSpace and hosts its managed cloud service.
Because the core product is open source, organizations can self-host ResourceSpace for free with no per-user license fees, or choose Montala's managed cloud hosting for a fully supported experience.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: ResourceSpace covers the core DAM workflow: upload, tag, search, and control access to images, video, and documents, with metadata schemas, custom fields, watermarking, and usage rights tracking.

Paid cloud tiers add AI-assisted automatic tagging, face detection, and natural-language search, plus SSO, API access, SFTP drop folders for bulk ingest, and public access portals for external sharing.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Self-hosting ResourceSpace is completely free with no license cost or user limits. A free cloud tier offers 10GB of storage for up to 10 users.

Paid cloud hosting runs from around $358/month (Team Cloud, 25 users) up to around $1,357/month (Enterprise Cloud, 500 users), with a custom-quoted Platinum Cloud tier for unlimited users and 50TB-plus storage.

features.title: Open-source, self-hostable DAM core
features.description: Download and run the full platform on your own servers with no license fee.

features.title: Managed cloud hosting option
features.description: Tiered cloud plans hosted in London Docklands data centers for teams that don't want to self-host.

features.title: AI-assisted tagging and search
features.description: Automatic tagging, face detection, and natural-language search on paid cloud tiers.

features.title: Metadata schemas and custom fields
features.description: Configure detailed metadata structures for organizing large asset libraries.

features.title: SSO and API access
features.description: Available on paid cloud tiers for enterprise integration and authentication.

features.title: Usage rights and licensing tracking
features.description: Track asset rights, licensing terms, and watermarking for compliance.

features.title: SFTP drop folder
features.description: Bulk ingest assets via SFTP rather than manual uploads.

features.title: Public access portals
features.description: Share assets externally through public-facing portals on Business tier and above.

pros: Free, unrestricted open-source self-hosting option
No per-user licensing costs for self-hosted deployments
Managed cloud option removes hosting and maintenance burden
Strong metadata, tagging, and rights-management features for enterprise DAM needs
Employee-owned, B Corp maintainer with a transparent company structure
cons: Self-hosting requires in-house technical resources to install, secure, and maintain
Cloud plan pricing is not fully published for all tiers; larger tiers need a custom quote
Interface is less modern-looking than some venture-backed commercial DAM competitors
AI features are limited primarily to paid cloud tiers
Smaller support and services team compared to larger commercial DAM vendors

faqs.question: Is ResourceSpace really free?
faqs.answer: Yes, ResourceSpace is open source and can be self-hosted for free with no license fee or user limits.

faqs.question: What is ResourceSpace used for?
faqs.answer: ResourceSpace is used as a digital asset management system to store, tag, search, and control access to images, video, and documents.

faqs.question: Who makes ResourceSpace?
faqs.answer: ResourceSpace is maintained by Montala Limited, a UK-based, employee-owned company founded by original creator Dan Huby.

faqs.question: Does ResourceSpace offer cloud hosting?
faqs.answer: Yes, Montala offers a free cloud tier plus paid Team, Business, Enterprise, and Platinum cloud hosting tiers.

faqs.question: How much does ResourceSpace cloud hosting cost?
faqs.answer: Paid cloud plans range from around $358/month for Team Cloud up to around $1,357/month for Enterprise Cloud, with custom Platinum pricing above that.

faqs.question: Does ResourceSpace have AI features?
faqs.answer: Yes, paid cloud plans include AI-assisted auto-tagging, face detection, and natural-language search.

faqs.question: Can I self-host ResourceSpace?
faqs.answer: Yes, ResourceSpace is open source and designed to be self-hosted on your own infrastructure.

faqs.question: Does ResourceSpace support single sign-on?
faqs.answer: Yes, SSO is available on the paid cloud hosting plans.

use_cases.description: Marketing and creative teams use managed cloud DAM to organize and distribute brand asset libraries.

use_cases.title: Rights-managed digital collections
use_cases.description: Museums and archives manage rights-tracked collections with public-facing access portals.

enrichment.short_description: Respond.io is an AI-powered business messaging platform that unifies WhatsApp, Messenger, Instagram, Telegram, and other channels into one inbox for sales and support teams.
enrichment.pricing_model: Tiered monthly or annual subscription based on users and monthly active contacts
enrichment.seo_title: Respond.io Review 2026: Pricing, Features and Alternatives
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Respond.io unifies WhatsApp, Messenger, Instagram and more into one AI-powered inbox. Compare 2026 pricing plans, features, pros/cons and FAQs.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Respond.io is a business messaging and customer conversation management platform founded in 2017 and headquartered in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, with a team of roughly 196 people as of 2026. The company has raised a total of about $71.3 million, including a $7 million Series A led by Headline and a $62.5 million Series B led by Camber Partners announced in June 2026 that it said would fund expansion into North America and Europe and support acquisitions. The product's core value proposition is unifying messaging channels, including the WhatsApp Business API, Facebook Messenger, Instagram DMs, Telegram, WeChat, LINE, Viber, SMS, email, and web chat, into a single shared inbox so sales, marketing, and support teams can manage every customer conversation from one place, with routing rules, team collision-avoidance, and reporting layered on top. Respond.io has increasingly positioned itself around AI: its AI Agents feature lets teams deploy AI-driven auto-responders and lead-qualification bots directly inside conversations, and AI Prompts and AI Assist features help human agents draft replies faster. Pricing is based on a combination of user seats and Monthly Active Contacts, or MACs, which are unique contacts a business messages within a billing period. As of 2026 there are four tiers: Starter at $79/month billed annually, or $99/month billed monthly, includes 5 users and unlimited MACs but excludes broadcasts, workflow automation, and AI Agents; Growth at $159/month billed annually, or $199/month billed monthly, includes 10 users, starts with 1,000 MACs, and unlocks broadcasts, workflow automation, AI Agents, Zapier and Make integrations, and a developer API, with overage billed at $12 per additional 100 MACs; Advanced at $279/month billed annually, or $349/month billed monthly, includes 10 users, adds SSO, Salesforce integration, multiple workspaces, and webhook or HTTP-request steps in workflows, with overage at $15 per 100 MACs; and Enterprise is custom-priced with unlimited users, higher API limits, and a dedicated success manager. All self-serve plans include a 7-day free trial with Growth-level features. Underlying Meta and WhatsApp messaging fees are billed separately by Meta on top of respond.io's own subscription. The platform is widely used by e-commerce brands, real estate agencies, and B2B sales teams in markets with high WhatsApp usage, such as Southeast Asia, Latin America, and the Middle East, that need to manage high message volumes without losing conversations across channels.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Respond.io is a business messaging platform founded in 2017 and headquartered in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, that unifies channels like WhatsApp, Messenger, Instagram, and Telegram into a single shared inbox for sales and support teams.
The company has raised roughly $71.3 million, including a $62.5 million Series B in June 2026, and has increasingly focused on AI Agents that automate conversation handling and lead qualification.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: The platform's core is an omnichannel inbox spanning WhatsApp Business API, Messenger, Instagram, Telegram, SMS, email, and web chat, with routing rules and team collision-avoidance to prevent duplicate replies.

AI Agents and AI Assist automate and accelerate replies, while workflow automation, broadcast messaging, and integrations with Zapier, Make, and Salesforce extend the platform for sales and marketing use cases.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Respond.io prices plans by user seats and Monthly Active Contacts (MACs). Starter costs $79/month annually with unlimited MACs but no automation; Growth costs $159/month annually with automation and AI Agents starting at 1,000 MACs; Advanced costs $279/month annually and adds SSO and Salesforce integration.
Overage fees apply once MAC limits are exceeded on Growth and Advanced, and Meta/WhatsApp messaging costs are billed separately from the respond.io subscription. Enterprise pricing is custom.

features.description: Prevent multiple agents from replying to the same conversation.

features.title: MAC-based contact management
features.description: Pricing and limits are based on Monthly Active Contacts messaged during a billing period.

features.title: Reporting and analytics
features.description: Dashboards for tracking response times, team performance, and conversation volume.

features.title: Integrations
features.description: Connects with Zapier, Make, Salesforce, webhooks, and a developer API.

pros: True omnichannel unification across most major messaging apps including WhatsApp
AI Agents and AI Assist reduce manual reply workload
Flexible workflow automation for lead routing and qualification
Unlimited MACs on the Starter plan for lower-volume teams
Strong fit for WhatsApp-heavy markets like Southeast Asia, Latin America, and the Middle East
cons: MAC-based pricing model can be confusing and lead to unexpected overage charges on higher tiers
Broadcasts, workflows, and AI Agents are locked out of the entry-level Starter plan
Meta/WhatsApp messaging fees are billed separately on top of the subscription cost
SSO and Salesforce integration require the Advanced tier
Free trial is limited to 7 days

faqs.question: What is respond.io used for?
faqs.answer: Respond.io is used to unify messaging channels like WhatsApp, Messenger, and Instagram into one inbox for sales and customer support teams.

faqs.question: How much does respond.io cost?
faqs.answer: Plans range from $79/month (Starter) to $279/month (Advanced) billed annually, with custom Enterprise pricing above that.

faqs.question: What is a Monthly Active Contact (MAC)?
faqs.answer: A MAC is a unique contact a business messages within a billing period, and it's the core usage metric respond.io's pricing is based on.

faqs.question: Does respond.io support WhatsApp?
faqs.answer: Yes, respond.io integrates with the official WhatsApp Business API alongside other messaging channels.

faqs.question: Does respond.io have AI features?
faqs.answer: Yes, it offers AI Agents for automated conversation handling and AI Assist to help agents draft replies.

faqs.question: Is there a free trial?
faqs.answer: Yes, respond.io offers a 7-day free trial with Growth-plan features.

faqs.question: Does respond.io integrate with Salesforce?
faqs.answer: Yes, Salesforce integration is available starting on the Advanced plan.

faqs.question: Where is respond.io based?
faqs.answer: Respond.io is headquartered in Kuala Lumpur, Malaysia, and was founded in 2017.

enrichment.short_description: Restaurant365 is an all-in-one restaurant management platform combining accounting, inventory, scheduling, and payroll built specifically for multi-unit restaurant operators.
enrichment.pricing_model: Custom quote-based subscription, priced per location per month
enrichment.starting_price: Custom quote; industry estimates put the Essential plan around $469-499/location/month
enrichment.company_size: Reported estimates vary; roughly 900+ employees at recent scale
enrichment.seo_title: Restaurant365 Review 2026: Pricing, Features and Alternatives
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Restaurant365 combines accounting, inventory, scheduling and payroll for restaurants. See 2026 pricing estimates, features, pros/cons and FAQs.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Restaurant365, often abbreviated R365, is an all-in-one restaurant management platform founded in 2011 and headquartered in Irvine, California, built specifically for multi-unit and multi-concept restaurant operators. The company has raised roughly $445 million in total funding across multiple rounds including a Series E, reaching unicorn status with a valuation reported at or above $1 billion; investors have included ICONIQ Capital, Bessemer Venture Partners, KKR, and L Catterton. Employee counts vary by source, with figures ranging from roughly 900 to several thousand depending on how contractors and recent growth are counted, reflecting the company's rapid scale-up serving a customer base reported at more than 40,000 restaurant locations. Restaurant365's platform is organized into four core product areas that can be purchased together: Accounting, covering AP automation, banking, financial reporting, and multi-entity consolidation; Inventory and Purchasing, covering recipe costing, vendor ordering, and waste tracking; Workforce Management, covering scheduling, time tracking, and labor forecasting; and Payroll and HR, covering payroll processing, hiring, and onboarding. Unlike many SaaS tools, Restaurant365 does not publish self-serve pricing; the official pricing page requires prospects to request a custom quote via sales call, demo, or email. Third-party sources and industry pricing guides commonly cite two named plan tiers: an Essential plan estimated around $469-499 per location per month covering POS Connect, R365 Accounting, AP Automation, Inventory, Scheduling, Recipe costing, Smart Labor, Smart Ordering, and Smart Prep; and a Professional plan estimated around $689-749 per location per month that adds Business Analytics, custom financial reporting, and fixed asset tracking. Both tiers reportedly include unlimited user seats per location, so pricing scales with the number of restaurant locations rather than headcount. Restaurant365 integrates with major point-of-sale systems including Toast, Square, and Clover, and with payroll and banking partners, and it markets itself as purpose-built for the accounting and operational complexity unique to restaurants, such as recipe-level food costing, prime cost tracking, and multi-location profit-and-loss consolidation, as opposed to generic accounting software like QuickBooks or general-purpose workforce tools. It primarily competes with other restaurant-specific back-office platforms such as MarginEdge, Toast's back-office modules, and Craftable.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Restaurant365 is a restaurant-specific back-office platform founded in 2011 and headquartered in Irvine, California, built to unify accounting, inventory, scheduling, and payroll for multi-unit restaurant operators.

Backed by roughly $445 million in funding and valued at over $1 billion, the company has scaled to serve tens of thousands of restaurant locations, though pricing and exact headcount are not fully public.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: The platform's core modules cover restaurant-specific accounting and AP automation, recipe-level inventory costing and purchasing, labor scheduling, and integrated payroll and HR, all built around multi-location financial consolidation.
It integrates with major POS systems like Toast, Square, and Clover, and adds business analytics dashboards and fixed asset tracking on its higher Professional tier.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Restaurant365 does not publish self-serve pricing; the official pricing page routes prospects to a custom quote via sales, demo, or email contact.

Third-party industry estimates commonly cite an Essential tier around $469-499 per location per month and a Professional tier around $689-749 per location per month, both reportedly with unlimited user seats per location.

Per-location pricing can be expensive for small independent restaurants
Implementation and onboarding can be complex given the breadth of modules
Reported employee and company-size figures vary significantly across data sources
Full value requires adopting multiple modules, which raises total cost

faqs.question: What is Restaurant365 used for?
faqs.answer: Restaurant365 is used as an all-in-one back-office platform for restaurant accounting, inventory, scheduling, and payroll.

faqs.question: How much does Restaurant365 cost?
faqs.answer: Pricing is custom-quoted, but industry estimates put plans in the range of roughly $469 to $749 per location per month depending on tier.

faqs.question: Does Restaurant365 include payroll?
faqs.answer: Yes, payroll and HR are available as part of Restaurant365's product suite.

faqs.question: Does Restaurant365 integrate with my POS?
faqs.answer: Yes, it integrates with major POS systems including Toast, Square, and Clover.

faqs.question: Is Restaurant365 for single restaurants or chains?
faqs.answer: It's built primarily for multi-unit and multi-concept restaurant operators, though single locations can also use it.

faqs.question: When was Restaurant365 founded?
faqs.answer: Restaurant365 was founded in 2011.

faqs.question: Where is Restaurant365 headquartered?
faqs.answer: Restaurant365 is headquartered in Irvine, California.

faqs.question: Does Restaurant365 offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: No public self-serve free trial is listed; prospects request a demo and custom quote from sales.

enrichment.short_description: Restic is a free, open-source command-line backup program that provides fast, encrypted, deduplicated backups to a wide range of local and cloud storage backends.
enrichment.pricing_model: Free and open source, no paid tiers
enrichment.seo_title: Restic Review 2026: Free Open-Source Backup Tool Guide
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Restic is a free, open-source command-line backup tool with encryption and deduplication. Learn about its features, supported backends, pros, cons and FAQs.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Restic is a free, open-source backup program distributed under the BSD 2-Clause license, first publicly released as version 0.1.0 in September 2015 and maintained since then by a small core team led by original author Alexander Neumann alongside an active community of contributors on GitHub, where the project restic/restic has accumulated tens of thousands of stars. As a single static binary written in Go, restic requires no separate server, database, or complex installation; it runs directly on Linux, macOS, Windows, FreeBSD, and OpenBSD from the command line. Its core technical design centers on content-defined chunking and deduplication, meaning restic only stores and transfers the parts of files that have actually changed since the last backup, which keeps repeated backups fast and storage-efficient even for large datasets. Every backup is encrypted client-side using AES-256 before it leaves the machine, so data stored in a restic repository is unreadable without the repository password, and restic includes built-in commands such as restic check and restore verification to confirm that backups are intact and actually restorable, addressing a common failure mode of backup tools where corrupted archives go undetected until a restore is needed. Restic supports a wide range of storage backends out of the box, including local and network-mounted disks, SFTP servers, a dedicated REST server, Amazon S3 and S3-compatible object storage, Backblaze B2, Microsoft Azure Blob Storage, Google Cloud Storage, and, through its rclone backend integration, dozens of additional cloud providers. Because restic has no company behind it and charges nothing for the software itself, there is no pricing model, subscription, or enterprise tier; costs to users come only from whatever storage backend they choose to point restic at, such as S3 or B2 storage fees. Restic is popular among developers, sysadmins, and self-hosters who want a scriptable, automatable backup solution for servers, NAS devices, and personal machines, and it is frequently used as the backup engine underneath higher-level tools and GUIs that add a user interface on top of restic's command-line core. Its lack of a native graphical interface, reliance on the command line, and community-only support model are the main trade-offs compared to commercial backup products, but for technical users these are outweighed by restic's combination of strong encryption, verified restores, deduplication efficiency, and true zero cost.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Restic is a free, open-source command-line backup program first released in September 2015 under the BSD 2-Clause license, written in Go and maintained by original author Alexander Neumann along with an active GitHub community.

It runs as a single static binary with no server or database required, and is designed for developers and sysadmins who need scriptable, verifiable, encrypted backups across a wide range of storage backends.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Restic's design centers on content-defined chunking for deduplication, client-side AES-256 encryption, and snapshot-based incremental backups, plus built-in commands to verify that backups are actually restorable.
It supports many storage backends including local disks, SFTP, a REST server, Amazon S3, Backblaze B2, Azure Blob Storage, Google Cloud Storage, and dozens more via its rclone integration.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Restic is entirely free and open source with no paid tiers, subscriptions, or company behind it. There is no cost for the software itself.
The only costs users incur come from whichever storage backend they choose to back up to, such as cloud object storage fees for S3 or Backblaze B2.

features.title: Client-side AES-256 encryption
features.description: All backup data is encrypted before it leaves the source machine.

features.title: Deduplication via content-defined chunking
features.description: Only changed portions of files are stored and transferred on each backup.

features.title: Snapshot-based incremental backups
features.description: Efficient, point-in-time snapshots that build on previous backups.

features.title: Built-in backup verification
features.description: Commands to check repository integrity and verify restores actually work.

features.title: Wide backend support
features.description: Works with local disks, SFTP, S3, Backblaze B2, Azure, Google Cloud, and more via rclone.

features.title: Cross-platform single binary
features.description: Runs on Linux, macOS, Windows, FreeBSD, and OpenBSD with no separate install.

features.title: Scriptable CLI
features.description: Suited for automation via cron jobs, systemd timers, or CI/CD pipelines.

features.title: Free and open source
features.description: Released under the BSD 2-Clause license with no cost or vendor lock-in.

pros: Completely free with no subscription or paid tiers
Strong encryption and deduplication built in by default
Wide range of supported storage backends, including via rclone
Verifiable backups reduce the risk of silent backup corruption
Lightweight single-binary install that's easy to automate via scripts or cron
cons: No official graphical user interface, command-line only
No official vendor support; relies on community forums and GitHub issues
Steeper learning curve for non-technical users compared to consumer backup apps
No built-in scheduling; must be paired with cron, systemd timers, or third-party wrappers
No managed storage included; users must bring and pay for their own backend

faqs.question: Is restic free?
faqs.answer: Yes, restic is completely free and open source with no paid tiers.

faqs.question: What license does restic use?
faqs.answer: Restic is released under the BSD 2-Clause license.

faqs.question: What storage backends does restic support?
faqs.answer: Local disks, SFTP, a REST server, Amazon S3, Backblaze B2, Azure Blob Storage, Google Cloud Storage, and many more via rclone.

faqs.question: Does restic encrypt backups?
faqs.answer: Yes, all data is encrypted client-side using AES-256 before it's stored.

faqs.question: Does restic have a graphical user interface?
faqs.answer: Not officially; restic is a command-line tool, though third-party GUIs exist.

faqs.question: What platforms does restic support?
faqs.answer: Linux, macOS, Windows, FreeBSD, and OpenBSD.

faqs.question: Who maintains restic?
faqs.answer: Restic was created by Alexander Neumann and is maintained by a small core team and community contributors on GitHub.

faqs.question: How do I schedule restic backups?
faqs.answer: Restic has no built-in scheduler; users typically pair it with cron, systemd timers, or third-party wrapper tools.

use_cases.title: Automated encrypted server backups

use_cases.title: Self-hosted NAS and home server backups
use_cases.description: Back up NAS or home server data to cloud object storage without a subscription.

use_cases.title: Scriptable developer and CI backups
use_cases.description: Integrate verifiable, scriptable backups into development workflows and CI/CD pipelines.

enrichment.short_description: Restream is a live-streaming platform that lets creators and businesses broadcast simultaneously to 30+ destinations like YouTube, Twitch, and LinkedIn from one dashboard or browser studio.
enrichment.pricing_model: Freemium subscription, monthly or annual billing
enrichment.starting_price: Free (2 channels); paid plans start at $19/month, or $16/month billed annually
enrichment.company_size: Approximately 65-121 employees, estimates vary by source
enrichment.seo_title: Restream Review 2026: Pricing, Features and Alternatives
enrichment.seo_meta_description: Restream lets you multistream live video to 30+ platforms at once. Compare 2026 pricing plans, features, pros/cons and FAQs for creators and teams.
enrichment.llm_readable_summary: Restream is a live-streaming and multistreaming platform founded in 2015 by Alexander Khuda and Andrew Surzhynskyi, now headquartered in Austin, Texas, with a team estimated at roughly 65 to 121 employees depending on the data source, and a total of about $56.5 million raised from investors including 7percent Ventures, Insight Partners, and Sapphire Ventures. The core product lets a single live video source be broadcast simultaneously to more than 30 destinations, including YouTube, Twitch, Facebook, LinkedIn, TikTok, and X, plus custom RTMP or SRT destinations, either by ingesting a stream from existing broadcasting software like OBS Studio or Streamlabs, or by producing directly in Restream's own browser-based tool, Restream Studio, which supports multi-guest video calls, screen sharing, and on-screen graphics without any local software install. Pricing follows a freemium model: the Free plan allows streaming to 2 of the 30+ supported channels at once with Restream branding included; Standard costs $19/month, or $16/month billed annually at $190/year, and removes branding while enabling 3 simultaneous channels and guest channels; Professional costs $49/month, or $39/month billed annually at $470/year, and raises the limit to 5 channels while adding full HD 1080p streaming, 4K Ultra HD recording, and simultaneous horizontal-plus-vertical dual-format streaming; Business costs $239/month, or $199/month billed annually at $2,390/year, and supports 8 channels, a branded website video player for up to 1,000 concurrent viewers, two included team seats, and co-producer mode for collaborative live production; and an Enterprise tier offers custom pricing for larger organizations. Optional Clips add-ons, ranging from $19 to $59 per month depending on plan, provide AI-generated short-form clips from live streams for repurposing on social media. All paid plans include cloud recording, stream analytics, and encrypted RTMPS delivery, with higher tiers unlocking more on-screen participants, up to 10, longer recording retention, and deeper team collaboration features. Restream is used heavily by independent streamers, podcasters, worship and church broadcasts, corporate town halls, and marketing teams running product launches or webinars who want to reach audiences across multiple platforms simultaneously rather than choosing just one, and it competes with tools like StreamYard, Streamlabs, and Ecamm Live in the browser-based live-production space.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Restream is a live-streaming and multistreaming platform founded in 2015 and headquartered in Austin, Texas, that lets a single live video source broadcast simultaneously to more than 30 platforms including YouTube, Twitch, Facebook, and LinkedIn.

It offers both integration with existing broadcasting software like OBS Studio and its own browser-based production tool, Restream Studio, for multi-guest live shows with no software install required.

long_form_blocks.heading: Key Features
paragraphs: Core features include simultaneous multistreaming, cloud recording, stream analytics, and encrypted RTMPS delivery, with higher tiers adding 1080p and 4K recording, dual-format horizontal-plus-vertical streaming, and a branded website video player.

Restream Studio supports multi-guest video calls, screen sharing, and on-screen graphics for browser-based live production, while an optional Clips add-on generates AI short-form clips from live streams.

long_form_blocks.heading: Pricing
paragraphs: Restream's Free plan supports 2 simultaneous channels with branding. Standard costs $19/month ($16/month annually) for 3 channels, Professional costs $49/month ($39/month annually) for 5 channels with 1080p and 4K recording, and Business costs $239/month ($199/month annually) for 8 channels plus a website player and team seats.
An Enterprise tier offers custom pricing, and an optional Clips add-on for AI-generated short-form video costs an additional $19-$59 per month depending on plan.

features.title: Simultaneous multistreaming
features.description: Broadcast one live stream to 30+ destinations at once, including YouTube, Twitch, Facebook, and LinkedIn.

features.description: Browser-based live production tool with multi-guest video calls and no software install required.

features.description: Embed a branded live player supporting up to 1,000 concurrent viewers on the Business plan.

features.title: AI-generated Clips
features.description: Optional add-on that creates short-form clips from live streams for social media repurposing.

features.title: Dual-format streaming
features.description: Stream in both horizontal and vertical formats simultaneously on Professional and above.

features.title: Encrypted RTMPS and SRT ingest
features.description: Secure stream delivery with SRT ingest available on the Business plan.

pros: Broadcasts to 30+ platforms simultaneously from one dashboard
Browser-based Restream Studio needs no separate broadcasting software
Free plan available for testing with 2 simultaneous channels
Cloud recording and analytics included on paid plans
Supports multi-guest, multi-camera live production
cons: Free plan includes Restream branding and a watermark
Channel limits scale with price, so reaching many destinations gets expensive
Clips, the AI short-form repurposing feature, is a separate paid add-on
Higher-tier features like SRT ingest and the website player require the Business plan
Employee and company-size figures vary across third-party data sources

faqs.question: What is Restream used for?
faqs.answer: Restream is used to broadcast a single live video stream to multiple platforms simultaneously, such as YouTube, Twitch, and Facebook at once.

faqs.question: Is Restream free?
faqs.answer: Yes, Restream offers a free plan supporting 2 simultaneous channels with Restream branding included.

faqs.question: How much does Restream cost?
faqs.answer: Paid plans start at $19/month (Standard) and go up to $239/month (Business), with custom Enterprise pricing above that.

faqs.question: What is Restream Studio?
faqs.answer: Restream Studio is a browser-based live production tool for multi-guest streams that requires no separate broadcasting software.

faqs.question: Can I stream to YouTube and Twitch at the same time with Restream?
faqs.answer: Yes, Restream supports simultaneous streaming to more than 30 destinations including YouTube and Twitch.

faqs.question: Does Restream record my streams?
faqs.answer: Yes, cloud recording is included on all paid plans.

faqs.question: Does Restream support 4K?
faqs.answer: 4K Ultra HD recording is available starting on the Professional plan.

faqs.question: When was Restream founded?
faqs.answer: Restream was founded in 2015 by Alexander Khuda and Andrew Surzhynskyi.
